title: Speaking: The Demanding Boss
learningGoal: Verbally command actions using causative verbs and answer questions using the passive voice.
instructions: You are a manager checking on the status of an event. You must answer questions using the passive voice (It has been done) and give orders using causative verbs (Get it done).

subTasks.prompt: Assistant: 'Sir, has the letter been written?' Reply: 'Yes, the letter has been written.' (Letter = Patra, Written = Likha gaya hai)

sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Yes, the letter has been written.

subTasks.prompt: Assistant: 'What about the guests?' Reply: 'Food is being fed to the guests.' (Guests = Mehmanon ko, Food = Khana, Being fed = Khilaya jaa raha hai)

sampleResponse.targetText: मेहमानों को खाना खिलाया जा रहा है।
sampleResponse.transliteration: Mehmanon ko khana khilaya jaa raha hai.
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Food is being fed to the guests.

subTasks.prompt: Assistant: 'The AC is broken.' Reply: 'Get the AC fixed today.' (AC = AC, Fixed = Theek, Get done = Karwao / Karwa deejiye)
Assistant: 'I can't do it.' Reply: 'Then get it done by someone else.' (Then = Toh, Someone else = Kisi aur se, Get done = Karwao)

sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Then get it done by someone else.

baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Yes, the letter has been written.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: मेहमानों को खाना खिलाया जा रहा है।
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Mehmanon ko khana khilaya jaa raha hai.
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Food is being fed to the guests.
Then get it done by someone else.

acceptableParaphrases: Using formal 'Karwa deejiye' instead of casual 'Karwao' is completely acceptable and encouraged.

criteria.name: Passive Failure
criteria.description: If user says 'Patra likha hai' instead of 'Likha gaya hai', deduct points. Must use the Jaana/Gaya passive.

criteria.name: Causative Failure
criteria.description: If user says 'Khanda khao' instead of 'Khilaya'. Or 'AC theek karo' instead of 'karwao'. Must use causatives.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Haan, patra likha gaya hai. Mehmanon ko khana khilaya jaa raha hai. Aaj hi AC theek karwao. Toh kisi aur se karwao.

feedbackRules.onPass: Fantastic! You are delegating like a true boss using causatives and passives.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the 'waa' infix. 'Karo' means you do it. 'Karwao' means you get someone else to do it. As a manager, you want 'Karwao'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. For sentence 2, notice how it combines First Causative (Khila - to feed) and Passive (jaa raha hai - is being gone/done).

audioAssets.audioTranscript: हाँ, पत्र लिखा गया है। मेहमानों को खाना खिलाया जा रहा है। आज ही एसी ठीक करवाओ। तो किसी और से करवाओ।

hintText: Make sure your lips round for the 'waa' sound in 'karwao'.
commonMistakes: Saying 'Theek karo' (Fix it yourself) instead of 'Theek karwao' (Get it fixed).
